quiz.title: SAT English: Unit 6 Quiz 12
quiz.subtitle: Mixed Conventions: Boundaries, Modifiers & Parallelism (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation

questions.question:
The geophysicist’s lecture focused on the seismic activity of the Pacific Ring of Fire; ________ she discussed the potential for utilizing geothermal energy as a sustainable power source in the region.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice C correctly places a comma after the conjunctive adverb 'furthermore' to separate it from the rest of the independent clause following the semicolon. Choice A and B use redundant or incorrect terminal punctuation, and Choice D omits the necessary comma entirely.

questions.question:
The newly installed filtration system at the chemical plant operates more efficiently than ________, resulting in a 15% reduction in overall energy consumption.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly uses the pro-verb 'did' to logically compare the action ('operates') of the new system to the past action of the old system. Choice A incorrectly uses a plural pronoun for a singular comparison, Choice C compares nouns imprecisely for an action-based context, and Choice D misuses 'that of' when a verb comparison is required.

questions.question:
Each of the manuscripts recovered from the archaeological site ________ a detailed account of the administrative practices used by the ancient civilization.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice D correctly uses the singular verb 'contains' to agree with the singular indefinite pronoun subject 'Each.' Choices A and B are incorrect progressive/participle forms, and Choice C uses a plural verb that disagrees with the singular subject.

questions.question:
The documentary filmmaker traveled to the remote village to interview the local elders, document the traditional weaving techniques, and ________ the community’s unique oral history.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice D correctly uses the base verb 'record' to maintain parallelism within the distributed infinitive structure: [to] interview, [to] document, and [to] record. Choice A introduces an unnecessary clause, Choice B is a noun phrase, and Choice C is a gerund, all of which break the grammatical symmetry.

questions.question:
Although the architect initially proposed a glass facade for the skyscraper, the city planning commission expressed concerns about ________ and the potential impact on local bird populations.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly uses the possessive pronoun 'its' to modify the noun 'reflection,' which aligns with formal grammatical standards. Choice A is informal and awkward in this construction, Choice C incorrectly uses a contraction ('it is'), and Choice D is wordy and disrupts parallelism with the following noun phrase 'the potential impact.'

questions.question:
The aerospace engineer noted that the turbine blades were showing signs of structural fatigue; ________ he recommended an immediate suspension of all test flights until the components could be replaced.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice A correctly places a comma after the conjunctive adverb 'consequently' following the semicolon. Choice B and C use redundant or incorrect punctuation, and Choice D omits the necessary comma to separate the transitional word from the main clause.

questions.question:
Unlike the current administration’s focus on short-term economic gains, the previous ________ prioritized long-term infrastructure investment and environmental sustainability.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice A correctly uses the singular noun 'administration' to logically complete the 'Unlike' comparison between two entities. Choice B is a possessive that creates a dangling comparison, Choice C is plural and mismatches the singular context, and Choice D introduces a verb that breaks the parallel noun structure required by 'Unlike.'

questions.question:
The committee of urban planners ________ that the proposed high-speed rail link will reduce commute times by nearly forty percent once the project is completed.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice C correctly uses the singular verb 'estimates' to agree with the collective noun 'committee' acting as a single unit. Choice A is a participle that cannot function as a main verb, Choice B is plural and shifts tense unnecessarily, and Choice D is plural, disagreeing with the singular subject.

questions.question:
While the museum's collection of Impressionist paintings is impressive, ________ that truly distinguishes the gallery from its international competitors.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
options: the rare medieval tapestries are what
it is the rare medieval tapestries
questions.explanation: Choice D correctly completes the dependent 'While' clause with a clear, emphatic independent clause. Choice A and C create sentence fragments or awkward constructions lacking a direct main verb relationship. Choice B is grammatically possible but fails to provide the necessary emphasis and syntactic clarity expected in formal academic prose.

questions.question:
To prepare for the rigorous demands of the ultramarathon, the athlete focused on building cardiovascular endurance, strengthening core muscles, and ________.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice C correctly uses the gerund phrase 'developing mental resilience' to maintain strict parallelism with the preceding gerund phrases 'building cardiovascular endurance' and 'strengthening core muscles.' Choice A introduces an infinitive, Choice B shifts to a passive clause, and Choice D uses a noun phrase, all of which disrupt the established grammatical symmetry.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can accurately apply punctuation rules, modifier placement, tense consistency, and parallel structure to maintain grammatical precision.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on whether introductory modifiers attach to the correct subject, verbs match their tense context, and list items share identical grammatical forms.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: commas alone cannot join independent clauses, possessive pronouns precede gerunds, and 'neither/nor' agrees with the closest noun.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Ask yourself: Who or what is performing the opening action? Does the verb match the time frame? Are parallel items structurally identical?
